title: Code Quality And Secret And Dependency Scanning Semantic Contract
description: Coverage-complete semantic contract for the code quality and secret and dependency scanning capability. Semantic information captured as a USF coverage record via the coverage record and equivalence audit; no external source code is imported and the V2 implementation language is an open decision.
aiGuidance: Coverage-complete draft grounded in semantic information (USF coverage record only; no source import; language open). Proof is hermetic/stale lineage, not current readiness. Enrich detail and publish fresh proof before generation; respect the hexagonal constraint in ADR 0005.

lifecycle.description: The security-gate lifecycle defines static rules and configuration, runs a static-analysis error gate, verifies SBOM baseline freshness, enforces SBOM license/security policy, validates code-scanning configuration and database behaviour, exposes a security-control route, and fails closed in authoritative mode. These are global-operator CI/build gates rather than tenant-facing runtime surfaces.

stateModel.description: State includes static-analysis rule severities, the SBOM baseline document and lock hash, SBOM policy audit output, code-scanning config/database/result state, security-route registration, and the global security-console clickthrough readiness. The state is configuration and evidence rather than owned tenant records.

permissions.description: The security control surface and the security-console clickthrough are operator/global-only and explicitly not tenant-safe, while the static gates are repository-controlled rather than runtime tenant permissions. There is no tenant-scoped access to the global code-quality consoles.

contracts.description: Contracts are the static-analysis gate, SBOM verify, SBOM policy, and code-scanning validate commands, the rule and code-scanning configuration files, and registration of an operator security route. These are CI/static contracts plus one global-operator route, not a tenant API.

validation.description: Validation checks static-analysis error findings, SBOM file integrity and lock hash, license/security policy, code-scanning binary/config/database behaviour with an authoritative fail-closed mode, and route registration. Where configured, a locally absent code-scanning tool may honestly skip rather than silently pass.

errorModel.description: Static-analysis error findings, a missing or stale SBOM, policy violations, missing code-scanning in authoritative mode, an invalid code-scanning configuration, or an absent route all fail non-zero. Authoritative mode fails closed so a missing gate cannot be treated as a pass.

auditModel.description: These static gates produce build evidence and reports rather than runtime audit rows; route access and provider/security changes are audited by their owning runtime capabilities. The gate output and evidence links are the durable record.

readinessModel.description: Readiness is gate success plus optional security-console/service readiness from platform service probes, with missing local tools classified honestly according to each script's mode. Readiness fails closed on missing or stale evidence rather than assuming success.

proof.description: Behaviour is exercised by static-analysis, SBOM verify/policy, code-scanning validate, security-test, and security-control-route checks asserting fail-closed gate state and route registration. Proof is hermetic/runtime lineage; fresh USF proof pending USF-101/USF-59.

uiSemanticDefinition.description: No distinct UI capability at the contract level (coverage matrix ui=0); UI is modelled separately and remains a future AI-customised deliverable.
